raw_consumer_statement:
I booked an XXXX rental in XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX for a short-term contract from XX/XX/25 to XX/XX/25. Upon arrival, I was given the wrong apartment and was told I would be moved to the correct apartment the next day. Upon arrival to the " correct '' apartment, the property was not as advertised : it was extremely small and narrow despite being advertised specifically as a spacious accommodation on the website, and the apartment did not match the pictures. I contacted XXXX immediately, and their representative verbally assured me I would receive a full refund if I relocated but told me I had to relocate that night for this to happen, so I booked a hotel for the night. The XXXX agent 's name was XXXX and the call took place on XX/XX/25 at XXXX XXXX Eastern Daylight Time and lasted for XXXX minutes ; a recording of that call should be available. 

Relying on XXXX 's assurance, I booked a different property at significant cost- over {$17000.00} for three months. Later, XXXX refused to honor their promise and did not issue the refund. I disputed the charge with my credit card company ( Citi ), but despite providing evidence, Citi ultimately ruled in favor of the merchant. I also asked XXXX to at least cancel the reservation that I had booked, but they refused and forced me to pay an additional {$1200.00} cancellation fee. 

I am now out {$5400.00} for the unusable first property plus the cancellation fee of {$1200.00} that XXXX forced me to pay despite only having stayed at this location for two nights, in addition to the {$17000.00} paid for the new booking. This represents a major financial loss, and XXXX conduct - promising a refund verbally, then refusing it - is misleading and places the entire financial burden on the consumer. I repeatedly told the XXXX agent that if a refund was not guaranteed, I would stay at the current property even though it did not appear as advertised on the website, but he assured me that I would be issued a refund. Citi failed to deliver on its promise to protect consumers. 

This case involves a consumer being misled by a global company, resulting in thousands of dollars in losses. Both XXXX and Citi failed to provide appropriate protection, and I am seeking regulatory review to ensure accountability and resolution.
